steps.text: Plate serial dilutions (e.g., 1.0 × 10-6 through 1.0 × 10-8) using pour or spread plating on TSA plates.
For pour plating, add molten TSA tempered to 45-55ºC to each plate, swirl, and allow agar to solidify.
Incubate plates for 24 ± 2 hours at 36 ± 1ºC and determine titer.

preconditions.text: Diluting the suspension prior to carrier inoculation will be necessary.

steps.text: Transfer third system control sterile carrier to tube of subculture medium.
Include a positive carrier control by placing one inoculated carrier into tube of secondary subculture medium.
Incubate controls and test sample tubes together for 21 days at 36 ± 1ºC.
Perform identification confirmation on a minimum of three positive carrier sets per test.
Perform confirmation using Gram stain and/or plating on TSA.
Alternatively, perform additional confirmation using VITEK, API analysis or comparable method.
Confirm growth from each positive carrier set if fewer than three positive carrier sets are available.
Select only one tube for confirmatory testing if both tubes are positive in carrier set.
Confirm at least 20% by Gram stain for test with 20 or more positive carrier sets.
Perform Gram stains from growth taken directly from positive tubes within 5-7 days of conducting the efficacy test.
Perform a neutralization confirmation test in advance or in conjunction with efficacy testing.
Add diluted inoculum to various sets of subculture media tubes.
Produce a spore preparation according to the procedure for amended nutrient agar.
Harvest growth from plates per the method, re-suspend pellet after final centrifugation in approximately 100 ml aqueous (40%) ethanol.
Determine spore count by serial dilution and plating on TSA.
Adjust the suspension to reach target titer of 1.0 × 108 to 1.0 × 109 CFU/mL.

constraints.text: All manipulations of the test organism are required to be performed in accordance with biosafety practices stipulated in the institutional biosafety regulations.
Personal protective clothing or devices are recommended during the handling of these items for purpose of activation, dilution, or efficacy testing.
A chemical fume hood or other containment equipment may be employed when appropriate during performing tasks with concentrated products.
Exact adherence to the method, good laboratory practices, and quality control (QC) are required for proficiency and validity of the results.

steps.text: Suspend 11.5 g nutrient agar in 495 mL water.
Add 5 mL 500 ppm MnSO4:H20.
Dissolve by boiling.
If necessary, adjust pH to 6.8 ± 0.2.
Autoclave for 15 min at 121ºC.
Pour agar into plates.
Suspend 25 g LB broth in 1 L water.
Mix well.
If necessary, adjust pH to 7.0 ± 0.2.
Dispense in bottles and autoclave for 15 min at 121°C.
Add 20 mL 1M NaOH to 1 L LB broth.
Mix well.
Dispense in bottles and autoclave for 15 min at 121°C.
Add 0.25 g of manganese sulfate to 500 mL water.
Filter sterilize for use.
Add 400 μL of ice-cold sterile water to fraction B tubes.
Add 400 μL of ice-cold LB broth to fraction C tubes.
Store these tubes in a refrigerator (2-5°C) until ready for use.
Label dilution microcentrifuge tubes (serial dilution blanks) with fraction letter (e.g. A, B, or C), carrier number (e.g. 1, 2, 3, etc.), and dilution (e.g., 10-1, 10-2, 10-3, etc.).
Prepare serial dilution blanks for A, B, and C fractions for all test carriers.
Add 900 μL of ice-cold LB broth to each dilution blank for test carriers.
Store these tubes in a refrigerator (2-5°C) until ready for use.
Prepare serial dilution blanks for A, B, and C fractions for all control carriers.
Add 900 μL of ice-cold LB broth to each dilution blank for control carriers.
Store these tubes in a refrigerator (2-5°C) until ready for use.
Sterilize forceps (two pair of forceps for each disinfectant/control tested plus 3 extra).
Prepare a 50 mL conical tube with the appropriate neutralizer (place it on ice on test day).
Turn on the recirculating chiller and water bath and allow them to reach 20 ± 1°C or the temperature specified.
On test day, prepare the disinfectant(s) and place ~1.5 mL of each disinfectant and water control into a microcentrifuge tube.
Be sure to prepare and use the disinfectant within its specified period.
If the disinfectant requires a dilution, a minimum of 1 mL of the product must be used.
Ready-to-use disinfectants are tested as received; no dilution is required.
Using sterile forceps, carefully place one inoculated carrier inside each microcentrifuge tube labeled fraction A.
Avoid touching the inoculated area of the carrier and the sides of the microcentrifuge tube. If the carrier touches the sides of the microcentrifuge tube, the carrier and tube must be discarded.
These forceps should not be used for any additional transfers, unless sterilized appropriately.
Place the fraction A tubes containing the carriers and the tubes containing the disinfectant(s) and water control into a chiller water bath equilibrated at 20 ± 1°C.
Allow the carriers, disinfectant(s), and water control to equilibrate for approx. 10 min.
Using a 1000 μL micropipette, add 400 μL of the disinfectant or 400 μL sterile water control at 15 or 30 ± 5 sec intervals to the appropriate microcentrifuge tubes (in triplicate).
Expose fraction A tubes in the chiller water bath for the appropriate exposure period.
Record information and raw data on the test forms and data sheets provided by the Study Director.
Populate electronic spreadsheets with the data from the hard copy data sheets.
Peer-review the populated electronic spreadsheets for accuracy.
Forward the reviewed data to the statistician for analysis.
Record the preparation of media and reagents on Media Preparation Sheets provided by the Study Director.
Utilize the services of Dr. Martin Hamilton for statistical analysis.
Conduct statistical analyses to produce estimates of the repeatability standard deviation (Sr) and reproducibility standard deviation (SR).
Plot raw data points for visual inspection to see trends and detect outliers or influential observations.
Analyze control carrier spore titers using an analysis of variance (ANOVA) two factor, nested, random effects model.
Calculate the log reduction (LR) value for each combination of test method and test treatment.
